subTopicTitle: Heart of Darkness (Conrad) — Part II — Fog & attack

items.prompt: Describe the significance of the fog in Part II of 'Heart of Darkness'.
keyPoints: Represents the unknown and fear of the unknown

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: Recall and explain the sequence of events during the attack on Marlow's steamboat.
keyPoints: The steamboat is enveloped in fog
Marlow blows the whistle to scare attackers

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: Identify and explain the roles of key characters present during the fog and attack scene.
keyPoints: Marlow as the captain, trying to navigate
The fireman and the pilgrims’ reactions

markingCriteria.full: Includes all key points with accurate detail
markingCriteria.partial: Includes 1-2 key points or lacks detail

items.prompt: Imagine you are Marlow during the attack. Describe your thoughts and actions in response to the fog and attack.
keyPoints: Focus on navigating through the fog
Decision to use the whistle as a deterrent

markingCriteria.full: Applies theory to scenario with insight and detail
markingCriteria.partial: Applies theory but lacks depth or detail
markingCriteria.none: Fails to apply theory or is irrelevant

items.prompt: Apply the theme of colonial fear of the unknown to the fog and attack scene.
keyPoints: Fog as a metaphor for colonial ignorance
Fear heightened by lack of visibility

markingCriteria.full: Applies theme to scenario with insight and detail
markingCriteria.partial: Applies theme but lacks depth or detail
markingCriteria.none: Fails to apply theme or is irrelevant

items.prompt: Apply the concept of 'otherness' to the fog and attack scene.
keyPoints: Natives as the 'other', unseen and unknown

markingCriteria.full: Applies concept with insight and detail
markingCriteria.partial: Applies concept but lacks depth or detail
markingCriteria.none: Fails to apply concept or is irrelevant

items.prompt: Evaluate the effectiveness of Conrad's use of fog as a literary device in Part II.

markingCriteria.full: Evaluates effectively with evidence and insight
markingCriteria.partial: Some evaluation but lacks evidence or depth
markingCriteria.none: Fails to evaluate or lacks relevance

items.prompt: Assess how the attack scene advances the novel's critique of colonialism.

markingCriteria.full: Assesses effectively with evidence and insight
markingCriteria.partial: Some assessment but lacks evidence or depth
markingCriteria.none: Fails to assess or lacks relevance

items.prompt: Evaluate the portrayal of fear and its impact on character decisions during the fog and attack.
keyPoints: Fear of the unknown drives irrational actions
Marlow's decision to use the whistle

markingCriteria.full: Evaluates effectively with evidence and insight
markingCriteria.partial: Some evaluation but lacks evidence or depth
